title: Cool Automations — n8n death-detection + pantry-camera, Nov 2025
html:
TL;DR

  • Two distinctly Yahir-flavored automation ideas:
    1. Death-detection final-message bot. An n8n workflow connected to Apple Watch / Whoop / Oura that watches for heart-rate-zero-for-5-minutes → sends parent/SOS contact a "did you die?" message → if yes, fires a mass farewell message to everyone you know via text/email/Snapchat with a custom {final farewell message}.
    2. AI fridge-and-pantry vision. Camera in fridge + pantry that understands current inventory → AI suggests meals based on what's available + your macro goals.

Pattern observed

Both automations sit at the intimate-data + death/health intersection — Yahir's automation imagination is biographical, not productivity-oriented.

Why it matters in the graph

  • Death-detection automation is the technical sister to:
    • 2025 07 18 slideshow death song (each friend's funeral song picked).
    • 2024 09 12 never forget you can do whatever the fuck you want ("you are going to die soon").
    • 2024 03 30 karma no tomorrow ("death is imminent").
    • The whole legacy question cluster.
  • The fridge-camera meal AI sits inside the Brotein family-of-products universe — same macro-tracking + automation theme. Could be a Brotein-branded extension.
  • n8n as the orchestration layer — first time Yahir specifies a workflow tool by name in the corpus. Worth tracking if n8n becomes his go-to.
  • Pairs with 2025 09 08 logging health data naviwell — Yahir is interested in wearable + clinical data flow in the health-coaching context.

Open questions

  • Has the death-detection workflow been prototyped, or stays as idea?
  • "Snapchat" as a delivery channel for final farewell — programmable? Likely no; would need API workaround.
